mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off selling in equities.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 55% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate profit margins and generate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% creates a high discount rate environment that suppresses equity valuations and triggers negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates healthy job creation without wage inflation pressures, supporting sustained corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing a consistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window or GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening phase signals economic contraction that rapidly erodes equity valuations and generates negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 40% of triggered instances, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS is stable or declining.
